overview.title: API Overview
overview.description: Welcome to the official Orders API documentation. This API allows technical partners to integrate their systems directly with our delivery platform.

items.title: High Performance
items.text: Optimized RESTful architecture with a 99.9% uptime SLA.

items.title: OAuth2 Security
items.text: Short-lived JWT tokens with automatic renewal.

items.title: Real-time Events
items.text: Webhooks for instant order event notifications.

items.title: Idempotency
items.text: All critical operations support idempotency keys.

items.title: Credentials
items.text: Obtain your `client_id` and `client_secret` from the partner dashboard.

items.title: OAuth2 Token
items.text: Exchange your credentials for an **access_token** via the auth endpoint.

items.title: Receive Orders
items.text: Set up a webhook or poll `GET /v2/orders?status=PENDING` periodically.

items.title: Update
items.text: Update order status as it progresses: `PREPARING → READY → DISPATCHED → DELIVERED`.

sections.title: API Versioning
sections.content: The API uses URL prefix versioning (`/v2`). The previous version (`/v1`) will be deprecated on **Dec 31, 2025**.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: The API uses OAuth 2.0 with the Client Credentials flow. All requests must include a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

sections.title: Obtaining an Access Token
sections.content: Make a `POST` request to the auth endpoint with your `client_id` and `client_secret`. The returned token is valid for **3600 seconds** (1 hour). Store it and renew before expiry.

sections.title: Credential Security
sections.content: Never expose your `client_secret` on the client side. Keep it exclusively on your server. If compromised, revoke it immediately from the partner dashboard.

sections.title: Using the Token
sections.code:
curl -X GET https://api.example.com/v2/orders \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json"

rows.description: Invalid, expired, or missing token.
Valid token but insufficient permissions for the requested resource.
Invalid or missing authentication parameters.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: The API provides two environments: Sandbox for development/testing and Production for live operations.

sections.title: Sandbox — Test Data
sections.content: In the Sandbox, test orders are generated automatically every 2 minutes. Use `client_id=test_partner_001` and `client_secret=test_secret_001` for authentication. **No real charges occur in Sandbox.**

sections.title: Production — Caution
sections.content: Operations in production affect real orders and customers. Test thoroughly in Sandbox before going live.

rate-limiting.title: Rate Limiting
rate-limiting.description: The API enforces per-partner rate limits to ensure stability and availability for all integrators.

sections.title: Limit Exceeded
sections.content: When the limit is exceeded, the API returns `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests`. Wait the time indicated in the `Retry-After` header before retrying.

rows.name: X-RateLimit-Limit
rows.description: Maximum requests allowed in the current period.

rows.name: X-RateLimit-Remaining
rows.description: Remaining requests in the current period.

rows.name: X-RateLimit-Reset
rows.description: Unix timestamp when the limit resets.

rows.name: Retry-After
rows.description: Seconds to wait before retrying (present on 429 responses).

sections.title: 429 Response Example
sections.code:
HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests
Content-Type: application/json
Retry-After: 30
X-RateLimit-Limit: 60
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1715000430

{
  "error": "rate_limit_exceeded",
  "message": "Rate limit reached. Please retry in 30 seconds.",
  "retry_after": 30
}

order-lifecycle.title: Order Lifecycle
order-lifecycle.description: Understand all possible order states and valid transitions between them.

rows.description: Order received. Awaiting partner confirmation (timeout: 5 min).
Partner confirmed receipt. Preparation not yet started.
Order is being prepared.
Order is ready for courier pickup.
Courier collected the order. In transit to customer.
Order successfully delivered. Final state.
Order cancelled by customer, partner, or system. Final state.

sections.title: Confirmation Timeout
sections.content: `PENDING` orders not confirmed within **5 minutes** are automatically cancelled. Implement webhooks or polling for immediate confirmation.

list-orders.title: List Orders
list-orders.description: Returns a paginated list of orders for the authenticated partner, with support for filtering by status, date, and other criteria.

rows.name: status
rows.description: Filter by order status: `PENDING`, `CONFIRMED`, `PREPARING`, `READY`, `DISPATCHED`, `DELIVERED`, `CANCELLED`.

rows.name: from
rows.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format. E.g.: `2024-01-15T00:00:00Z`.

rows.name: to
rows.description: End date in ISO 8601 format. E.g.: `2024-01-15T23:59:59Z`.

rows.name: page
rows.description: Page number (starts at 1). Default: `1`.

rows.name: page_size
rows.description: Items per page. Max: `100`. Default: `20`.
Invalid query parameters.
Missing, invalid or expired access token.
Partner lacks read permissions for orders.
Rate limit reached. Check `Retry-After` header.
Internal server error. Retry after a moment.

get-order.title: Get Order by ID
get-order.description: Returns the complete details of a specific order, including items, delivery address, and status history.

rows.name: order_id
rows.description: Unique order identifier in the format `ord_xxxxxxxx`.
Missing or invalid token.
Order belongs to a different partner.
Order with the given ID not found.

confirm-order.title: Confirm Order
confirm-order.description: Confirms receipt of an order by the partner. Must be called within 5 minutes of order creation.

sections.title: Mandatory Confirmation
sections.content: `PENDING` orders not confirmed in **5 minutes** are automatically cancelled. Confirm immediately upon receiving a new order.

rows.name: estimated_time
rows.description: Estimated preparation time in **minutes** (min: 5, max: 120).

rows.name: message
rows.description: Optional message for the customer (max 200 characters).

rows.name: idempotency_key
rows.description: Idempotency key to prevent duplicate confirmations (UUID v4 recommended).

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X POST "https://api.example.com/v2/orders/ord_9f3a2b1c/confirm" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Idempotency-Key: 7f9c2a3b-1234-4abc-8def-56789abcdef0" \
  -d '{
    "estimated_time": 25,
    "message": "Your order is being prepared!"
  }'

rows.description: `estimated_time` missing or out of range (5–120 min).
Order was already confirmed.
Order is not in `PENDING` status.
Order expired before confirmation.

update-order-status.title: Update Order Status
update-order-status.description: Updates the status of a confirmed order through the lifecycle: PREPARING → READY → DISPATCHED → DELIVERED.

rows.name: status
rows.description: New order status: `PREPARING`, `READY`, `DISPATCHED`, `DELIVERED`.

rows.name: notes
rows.description: Internal notes about the transition (not visible to customer).

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X PUT "https://api.example.com/v2/orders/ord_9f3a2b1c/status" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": "READY",
    "notes": "Order packaged and awaiting courier"
  }'

rows.description: Invalid or missing status.
Order not found.
Invalid status transition.

cancel-order.title: Cancel Order
cancel-order.description: Cancels an active order. Only possible for orders in PENDING, CONFIRMED, or PREPARING status.

sections.title: Irreversible Action
sections.content: Cancellation is permanent. The customer is notified immediately and a refund is processed automatically within 5 business days.

rows.name: reason
rows.description: Reason code: `OUT_OF_STOCK`, `STORE_CLOSED`, `ITEM_UNAVAILABLE`, `PARTNER_REQUEST`, `OTHER`.

rows.name: description
rows.description: Additional description (max 500 characters).

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X DELETE "https://api.example.com/v2/orders/ord_9f3a2b1c" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "reason": "ITEM_UNAVAILABLE",
    "description": "Special Burger is currently out of stock."
  }'

rows.description: Invalid or missing cancellation reason.
Order not found.
Order cannot be cancelled in its current status.

webhook-intro.title: Webhook Introduction
webhook-intro.description: Webhooks allow you to receive real-time notifications when events occur on the platform, eliminating the need for constant polling.

sections.title: Recommended for Production
sections.content: Webhooks are more efficient than polling and guarantee minimal latency for receiving new orders. In production, **always prefer webhooks** over polling.

items.title: Registration
items.text: Register your webhook URL with the desired events.

items.title: Challenge
items.text: We send a `GET` with a `challenge` parameter. Your server must return the challenge value.

items.title: Verification
items.text: After successful verification, your webhook is active and will receive events.

items.title: Validation
items.text: Validate the HMAC-SHA256 signature in the `X-Webhook-Signature` header on each payload.

sections.title: Signature Validation (Node.js)

order-events.title: Order Events
order-events.description: List of all order events that can be sent via webhook.

rows.description: New order available for confirmation. Main event for integrations.
Order cancelled due to confirmation timeout.
Order cancelled by customer, partner, or system.
Delivery successfully confirmed.

error-codes.title: Error Codes
error-codes.description: Complete reference of error codes returned by the API and how to handle them.

sections.title: Error Response Structure

rows.description: Successful request.
Resource created successfully.
Invalid parameters. Check `details` in the response body.
Missing, invalid or expired token. Refresh the access_token.
No permission for this resource. Check token scopes.
Resource not found.
Conflict with current resource state.
Valid entity but business rule violated (e.g., invalid status transition).
Rate limit reached. Wait the time in `Retry-After`.
Internal error. Include `request_id` when reporting to support.
Service temporarily unavailable. Implement retry with exponential backoff.
